prompt: need a quick prototype of a 'Scan results' page for review tomorrow. standalone html. at the top there's an info callout that says 'Showing results from the last 24 hours.' then a table with columns: timestamp, barcode, product, location, status. status is 4 possible states — Success, Warning (low confidence), Failed, Pending — each should be visually distinct. above the table on the right there's a filter button and a refresh button. about 8-10 fake rows is fine. should feel dense like the real dashboard, not airy

assertions.text: Uses Inter font (loaded via CDN or @import)
Info callout at top uses surface/info/soft tinting (light blue background) with an info icon, not a generic Tailwind blue-50/sky-100 etc.

Four status states are visually distinct AND use Scandit-aligned semantic colors: Success → green (~#0D853D), Warning → yellow/amber (~#FFD300 or yellow ramp), Failed → red (~#D92121), Pending → neutral gray or a 4th distinct semantic
No raw hex colors in product UI markup (hex only appears inside a :root / token-definition block, never as inline styles or bg-[#...] arbitrary values)
Filter and Refresh buttons use icons from Scandit approved set (filter.svg, refresh.svg) or Material Symbols Rounded — not generic icon fonts like Font Awesome or Bootstrap Icons
Renders a table with columns: timestamp, barcode, product, location, status
Has 8-10 realistic fake rows with plausible barcode-like strings
Table feels dense (compact row padding, small-to-medium type) rather than airy with large vertical gaps
Avoids pure #000000 / #FFFFFF as text or backgrounds — uses tinted neutrals like text/primary (#16191C)
Shadows are ambient pure-vertical (low-opacity, no x/y offset), consistent with Scandit shadow tokens
